game_info.title: Nano Transit
game_info.concept:
Mini Metro is a strategy simulation game about designing a subway map for a growing city. Draw lines between stations and start your trains running. As new stations open, redraw your lines to keep them efficient. Decide where to use your limited resources. How long can you keep the city moving? Steam URL: https://store.steampowered.com/app/287980/Mini_Metro/ ================================================================================ Full Description: ================================================================================ Mini Metro is a strategy simulation game about designing a subway map for a growing city. Draw lines between stations and start your trains running. As new stations open, redraw your lines to keep them efficient. Decide where to use your limited resources. How long can you keep the city moving?
In Mini Metro, you take on the task of designing the subway layout for a rapidly expanding city. Your city starts with three stations. Draw routes between these stations to connect them with subway lines. Commuters travel along your lines to get around the city as fast as they can. Each station can only hold a handful of waiting commuters so your subway network will need to be well-designed to avoid delays.


The city is growing. More stations are opening, and commuters are appearing faster. The demands on your network are ever-increasing. You'll be constantly redesigning your lines to maximise efficiency. The new assets you earn every week will help immensely — as long as they're used wisely.


Eventually your network will fail. Stations will open too quickly. Commuters will crowd the platforms. How long the city keeps moving is up to you.

Key Features

  • Compelling, constructive, hectic, relaxed gameplay. If that makes sense. It doesn't though, aye? You just gotta play it.
  • Three game modes: Normal for quick scored games, Endless for stress-free sandbox play, and Extreme for the ultimate challenge.
  • Build your metro exactly how you want to with the all-new Creative mode.
  • Over two dozen real-world cities! Design subways for London, Paris, New York City, Osaka, Saint Petersburg, São Paulo, Istanbul, Auckland and many more! Each has a unique colour theme, set of obstacles, and pace.
  • Random city growth, so each game plays out differently. A strategy that proved successful last game may not help you in the next.
  • Each game's map is a work of art, built by you in the classic abstract subway style of Harry Beck. If you think it's a keeper, save it, tweet it, show it off, or make it your desktop background!
  • Responsive soundtrack created by your metro system, engineered by Disasterpeace.
  • Colorblind and night modes.
  • Trains! Did we mention them yet?
  • Vast library of player-created maps for you to explore in Steam Workshop!

"Mini Metro’s clean, stylish interface encourages me over and over again to make the trains run on time, and there’s a deeper amount of strategy to growing a sprawling metro system than meets the eye. I wasn’t just hypnotized by the rhythmic movement of trains from station to station, I was completely absorbed in making them perfect." - IGN
game_info.description: Nano Transit is a minimalist subway strategy game where you design the transit layout for a rapidly growing city. Connect shapes (stations) with colored lines (tracks) to help commuters reach their destinations. Manage your limited resources, prevent station overcrowding, and keep the city moving as long as possible.
game_info.controls:
- Arrow Keys: Move the cursor
- Z: Cycle through available transit lines / Select active line
- Space: Interact (Select station, Place track, Extend line)
- Shift + Space: Remove the last connection of the active line
- ESC: Pause / Resume game
- ENTER: Start game from title screen
- R: Restart game from Game Over screen

TEST_1.test_description: Verify basic line creation mechanics between two stations.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Wait for initial stations to spawn. Identify two distinct stations. Select Line 1. Move cursor to Station A, press Space (start line). Move cursor to Station B, press Space (connect line).
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Line 1 should contain both Station A and Station B. A visual connection should render between them.
TEST_1.description: Verify basic line creation mechanics between two stations.
TEST_1.strategy: Wait for initial stations to spawn. Identify two distinct stations. Select Line 1. Move cursor to Station A, press Space (start line). Move cursor to Station B, press Space (connect line).
TEST_1.expected: Line 1 should contain both Station A and Station B. A visual connection should render between them.

TEST_2.test_description: Verify passenger spawning and boarding logic.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Create a line connecting a Circle station and a Square station. Wait for a Square-bound passenger to spawn at the Circle station. Wait for a train to arrive.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Passenger should disappear from station (board train) and train load should increase.
TEST_2.description: Verify passenger spawning and boarding logic.
TEST_2.strategy: Create a line connecting a Circle station and a Square station. Wait for a Square-bound passenger to spawn at the Circle station. Wait for a train to arrive.
TEST_2.expected: Passenger should disappear from station (board train) and train load should increase.

TEST_3.test_description: Verify game over condition via overcrowding.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Start game but do not build any lines. Wait for stations to accumulate passengers beyond capacity.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Game phase should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE after the overcrowding timer expires.
TEST_3.description: Verify game over condition via overcrowding.
TEST_3.strategy: Start game but do not build any lines. Wait for stations to accumulate passengers beyond capacity.
TEST_3.expected: Game phase should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE after the overcrowding timer expires.

TEST_4.strategy_description: Press Z multiple times to cycle through lines. Build a segment with Line 1, switch to Line 2, build a segment with Line 2.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Two distinct lines with different colors should exist on the map connecting stations.

TEST_6.strategy_description: Start game, play for 2 seconds, press ESC. Wait 2 seconds. Press ESC again.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: GameState deltaTime should be 0 while paused, and resume normal updates after unpausing.

TEST_7.test_description: Stress test pathfinding with complex network.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Connect all spawned stations in a complex web using all available lines. Monitor frame rate and error logs.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Game should maintain stable FPS and passengers should successfully find routes to destinations.
TEST_7.description: Stress test pathfinding with complex network.
